## Callbacks

All callbacks are optional. Define any in `main.lua` and the engine calls them automatically.

```lua
function luna.load()  -- Called once after the script is loaded.
function luna.update( dt : number )  -- Called every frame; dt = elapsed seconds.
function luna.draw()  -- Called every frame for rendering.
function luna.keypressed( key : string, scancode : string, isrepeat : boolean )  -- Key press event.
function luna.keyreleased( key : string, scancode : string )  -- Key release event.
function luna.textinput( text : string )  -- Unicode character typed.
function luna.mousepressed( x : number, y : number, button : number )  -- Mouse button press.
function luna.mousereleased( x : number, y : number, button : number )  -- Mouse button release.
function luna.wheelmoved( x : number, y : number )  -- Mouse wheel scroll.
function luna.gamepadpressed( id : number, button : string )  -- Gamepad button press.
function luna.gamepadreleased( id : number, button : string )  -- Gamepad button release.
function luna.gamepadaxis( id : number, axis : string, value : number )  -- Gamepad axis; value in -1..1.
function luna.joystickadded( id : number )  -- Gamepad connected.
function luna.joystickremoved( id : number )  -- Gamepad disconnected.
function luna.touchpressed( id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch begin.
function luna.touchmoved(  id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch move.
function luna.touchreleased(id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch end.
function luna.focus( focused : boolean )  -- Window focus change.
function luna.visible( visible : boolean )  -- Window show/hide.
function luna.resize( w : number, h : number )  -- Window resized.
function luna.quit()  -- Return true to cancel quit.
function luna.errorhandler( msg : string )  -- Unhandled Lua error.
```

```lua
luna.timer.getTime()  -- @return any
```

### `mlua`

```lua
mlua:after( delay : number, func : function )  -- Calls a Lua function after the given delay in seconds
mlua:cancel( id : integer )  -- Cancels a scheduled timer callback
mlua:cancelAll()  -- Cancels and removes every pending timer entry from this scheduler
mlua:cancelNamed( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Cancels a specific named timer that was scheduled on this scheduler

mlua:pause( id : integer )  -- @param id : integer
mlua:resetEvent( id : integer )  -- Resets the elapsed time of the given timer so it fires again after its full interval
mlua:resume( id : integer )  -- Resumes a paused scheduler, allowing its callbacks to fire again
mlua:setInterval( id : integer, interval : number )  -- Changes the repeat interval of an existing timer without recreating it
mlua:setTimeScale( scale : number )  -- @param scale : number
mlua:update( dt : number )  -- Advances all pending timers by dt seconds
```

### `Body`

```lua

luna.particle.getAlphas()  -- Returns the alpha keyframes as a table of numbers (0–1)

luna.particle.getDirection()  -- Returns the emission direction in radians

luna.particle.getEmitterLifetime()  -- Returns the emitter lifetime in seconds (-1 means infinite)
luna.particle.getGravity()  -- Returns the gravity vector (gx, gy) in pixels per second squared
luna.particle.getInsertMode()  -- Returns the current particle insertion mode string
luna.particle.getLinearAcceleration()  -- Returns the linear acceleration range as (xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax)
luna.particle.getLinearDamping()  -- Returns the linear damping range (min, max)
luna.particle.getOffset()  -- Returns the draw offset (ox, oy) in pixels
luna.particle.getParticleLifetime()  -- Returns the particle lifetime range (min, max) in seconds
luna.particle.getPosition()  -- Returns the emitter world position (x, y)
luna.particle.getRadialAcceleration()  -- Returns the radial acceleration range (min, max)
luna.particle.getRelativeMode()  -- Returns the relative mode string
luna.particle.getRotation()  -- Returns the initial rotation range (min, max) in radians
luna.particle.getSizeVariation()  -- Returns the size variation factor (0–1)
luna.particle.getSizes()  -- Returns the particle size keyframes as a table of numbers
luna.particle.getSpeed()  -- Returns the particle speed range (min, max) in pixels per second
luna.particle.getSpin()  -- Returns the spin range (min, max) in radians per second
luna.particle.getSpinVariation()  -- Returns the spin variation factor (0–1)
luna.particle.getSpread()  -- Returns the emission spread angle in radians
luna.particle.getTangentialAcceleration()  -- Returns the tangential acceleration range (min, max)
luna.particle.getTexture()  -- Returns the texture handle assigned to this particle system, or nil if none is set
luna.particle.hasRelativeRotation()  -- Returns whether relative rotation is currently enabled
luna.particle.isActive()  -- Returns true if the emitter is actively emitting particles
luna.particle.isEmpty()  -- Returns true if there are no live particles in the system
luna.particle.isFull()  -- Returns true if the particle buffer is at maximum capacity
luna.particle.isPaused()  -- Returns true if the emitter is currently paused
luna.particle.isStopped()  -- Returns true if the emitter is stopped
luna.particle.moveTo()  -- Moves the emitter to (x, y), smoothly adjusting in-flight particles
luna.particle.newSystem()  -- Creates and returns a new particle system with optional initial configuration
luna.particle.pause()  -- Pauses particle emission without clearing live particles
luna.particle.release()  -- Frees the particle system and all its resources, invalidating the handle
luna.particle.reset()  -- Resets the emitter to its initial state, clearing all live particles
luna.particle.setAlphas()  -- Sets alpha transparency keyframes over the particle lifetime as variadic numbers (0–1)
luna.particle.setBufferSize()  -- Sets the maximum number of live particles the buffer can hold
luna.particle.setColors()  -- Sets color keyframes over the particle lifetime as variadic {r, g, b, a} tables
luna.particle.setDirection()  -- Sets the emission direction in radians (0 = right, π/2 = down)
luna.particle.setEmissionArea()  -- Sets the area distribution, dimensions, and angle for the particle spawn area
luna.particle.setEmissionRate()  -- Sets the continuous emission rate in particles per second
luna.particle.setEmissionShape()  -- Sets the area shape from which new particles are spawned. Supported shapes: "point", "circle", "rectangle", "ring", "line", "cone", "star", "spiral"
luna.particle.setEmitterLifetime()  -- Sets the emitter total lifetime in seconds. Use -1 for infinite
luna.particle.setGravity()  -- Sets the gravity vector applied to each particle every simulation step
luna.particle.setInsertMode()  -- Sets how new particles are inserted into the buffer: "top", "bottom", or "random"
luna.particle.setLinearAcceleration()  -- Sets the linear acceleration range applied to each particle per second
luna.particle.setLinearDamping()  -- Sets the linear damping range that slows particle velocity over time
luna.particle.setOffset()  -- Sets the draw offset applied to each particle relative to the emitter origin
luna.particle.setParticleLifetime()  -- Sets the minimum and maximum lifetime for each particle in seconds
luna.particle.setPosition()  -- Sets the emitter world position
luna.particle.setQuads()  -- Sets the sub-image quads used when the texture contains animated particle frames
luna.particle.setRadialAcceleration()  -- Sets the radial acceleration range applied toward or away from the emitter
luna.particle.setRelativeMode()  -- Sets whether particles stay attached to the emitter ("attached") or detach on spawn ("detached")
luna.particle.setRelativeRotation()  -- Enables or disables particle rotation relative to its velocity direction
luna.particle.setRotation()  -- Sets the initial rotation range for new particles in radians
luna.particle.setSizeVariation()  -- Sets the random size variation factor applied on spawn, clamped to 0–1
luna.particle.setSizes()  -- Sets the particle size keyframes over the particle lifetime as variadic numbers
luna.particle.setSpeed()  -- Sets the initial particle speed range in pixels per second
luna.particle.setSpin()  -- Sets the angular velocity (spin) range for particles in radians per second
luna.particle.setSpinVariation()  -- Sets the random spin variation factor applied on spawn, clamped to 0–1
luna.particle.setSpread()  -- Sets the emission spread angle for new particles
luna.particle.setTangentialAcceleration()  -- Sets the tangential acceleration range applied perpendicular to the radial direction
luna.particle.setTexture()  -- Assigns a texture to use when rendering particles
luna.particle.start()  -- Starts or resumes particle emission
luna.particle.stop()  -- Stops particle emission and clears all live particles
luna.particle.update()  -- Advances the particle simulation by dt seconds, spawning and aging particles
```

## `luna.ai` {#ai}

> Registers the `luna.ai.*` game AI toolkit API.
>
> Provides factory functions and UserData wrappers for AI subsystems:
> AIWorld, Agent, Blackboard, StateMachine, BehaviorTree, SteeringManager,
> QLearner, UtilityAI, GOAPPlanner, InfluenceMap,
> Squad, and CommandQueue.

*Coverage: 119/119 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.ai.newAction( callback : function ) -> Action  -- Creates a BT action leaf node with a Lua callback
luna.ai.newBehaviorTree() -> BehaviorTree  -- New behavior tree
luna.ai.newBlackboard() -> Blackboard  -- New blackboard
luna.ai.newCommandQueue() -> CommandQueue  -- New command queue
luna.ai.newCondition( callback : function ) -> Condition  -- Creates a BT condition leaf node with a Lua predicate
luna.ai.newGOAPPlanner() -> GOAPPlanner  -- New goap planner
luna.ai.newInfluenceMap( w : integer, h : integer, cs : number ) -> InfluenceMap  -- New influence map
luna.ai.newInverter() -> Inverter  -- Creates a BT inverter decorator (swaps success/failure)
luna.ai.newParallel( sp : string?, fp : string? ) -> Parallel  -- Creates a BT parallel node (ticks all children simultaneously)
luna.ai.newQLearner( sc : integer, ac : integer ) -> QLearner  -- Creates a tabular Q-learning agent
luna.ai.newRepeater( count : integer? ) -> Repeater  -- Creates a BT repeater decorator (repeats child N times, 0=infinite)
luna.ai.newSelector() -> Selector  -- Creates a BT selector node (tries children until one succeeds)
luna.ai.newSequence() -> Sequence  -- Creates a BT sequence node (runs children until one fails)
luna.ai.newSquad( name : string ) -> Squad  -- Creates a named squad for group formation positioning
luna.ai.newStateMachine() -> StateMachine  -- New state machine
luna.ai.newSteeringManager() -> SteeringManager  -- New steering manager
luna.ai.newSucceeder() -> Succeeder  -- Creates a BT succeeder decorator (always returns success)
luna.ai.newUtilityAI() -> UtilityAI  -- New utility ai
luna.ai.newWorld() -> World  -- Creates a new AI world container that owns agents and a global blackboard
```

## `luna.data` {#data}

> Registers the `luna.data.*` LÖVE2D-compatible binary data API.
>
> Provides the Lua-side `luna.data` namespace with functions that mirror the
> LÖVE2D `love.data.*` surface: `pack`, `unpack`, `getPackedSize`,
> `compress`, `decompress`, `encode`, `decode`, `hash`, `newByteData`,
> and `newDataView`.

*Coverage: 24/24 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.data.compress( format_str : string, raw_data : string, level : integer? )  -- Compresses data using the given algorithm (''deflate'', ''gzip'', ''lz4'')
luna.data.decode( format_str : string, encoded : string )  -- Decodes encoded text back to binary using the given format (''base64'', ''hex'')
luna.data.decompress( format_str : string, compressed_data : string )  -- Decompresses data using the given algorithm (''deflate'', ''gzip'', ''lz4'')
luna.data.encode( format_str : string, raw_data : string )  -- Encodes binary data using the given format (''base64'', ''hex'')
luna.data.encodeToml( tbl : table )  -- Encodes a Lua table as a TOML string
luna.data.getPackedSize( format : string )  -- @param format : string
luna.data.hash( algo_str : string, raw_data : string )  -- @param algo_str : string
luna.data.newByteData( value : any )  -- Creates a new mutable byte buffer of the given size or from a string
luna.data.newDataView( data : string, offset : integer?, size : integer? ) -> DataView  -- Creates a read-only windowed view into a byte string
luna.data.pack( format : string )  -- Packs values into a binary byte string using the LÖVE2D format string
luna.data.parseToml( toml_string : string )  -- Parses a TOML string and returns a Lua table representation
luna.data.read( format : string, data : string, offset : integer? )  -- Reads values using the Luna2D Binary Pack Format
luna.data.size( format : string ) -> integer  -- @param format : string
luna.data.unpack( format : string, raw_data : string, offset : integer? )  -- Unpacks values from a LÖVE2D-format binary byte string
luna.data.write( format : string )  -- Writes values using the Luna2D Binary Pack Format (space-separated type tokens)
```

### `DataView`

Represents a data view Lua object.

```lua
DataView:getDouble()  -- Reads a 64-bit IEEE 754 double at the given byte offset
DataView:getFloat()  -- Reads a 32-bit IEEE 754 float at the given byte offset
DataView:getInt16()  -- Reads a signed 16-bit little-endian integer at the given byte offset
DataView:getInt32()  -- Reads a signed 32-bit little-endian integer at the given byte offset
DataView:getInt8()  -- Reads a signed 8-bit integer at the given byte offset
DataView:getSize()  -- Returns the total size of the underlying data buffer in bytes
DataView:getUInt16()  -- Reads an unsigned 16-bit little-endian integer at the given byte offset
DataView:getUInt32()  -- Reads an unsigned 32-bit little-endian integer at the given byte offset
DataView:getUInt8()  -- Reads an unsigned 8-bit integer at the given byte offset
```

## `luna.scene` {#scene}

> Registers the `luna.scene.*` scene stack, registry, data store, and depth-sorter API.
>
> The scene stack manages a LIFO stack of Lua scene tables with optional
> visual transitions. Scene tables are stored via `mlua::RegistryKey` and
> identified by `SceneId`. Inter-scene data is stored similarly.

*Coverage: 26/28 items documented (93%)*

```lua
luna.scene.clear()
luna.scene.draw()
luna.scene.getCurrent()  -- @return any
luna.scene.getData( key : string )  -- @param key : string
luna.scene.getRegistered( name : string )  -- @param name : string
luna.scene.getRegisteredNames()  -- @return any
luna.scene.getStackSize()  -- @return any
luna.scene.getTransitionProgress()  -- @return any
luna.scene.hasData( key : string )  -- @param key : string
luna.scene.hasRegistered( name : string )  -- @param name : string
luna.scene.isEmpty() -> boolean  -- @return boolean
luna.scene.isTransitioning()  -- @return any
luna.scene.newDepthSorter() -> DepthSorter  -- New depth sorter
luna.scene.pop( transition : string?, duration : number? )  -- Removes an item
luna.scene.popTo( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Performs the pop to operation
luna.scene.push( scene : table, transition : string?, duration : number?, params : any? )  -- Pushes a scene onto the stack
luna.scene.registerScene( name : string, scene : table )  -- Register scene
luna.scene.removeData( key : string )  -- Removes the data from this scene
luna.scene.setData( key : string, value : any )  -- =======================================================================
luna.scene.switchTo( scene : table, transition : string?, duration : number?, params : any? )  -- Replaces the top scene with a new one
luna.scene.unregisterScene( name : string )  -- Unregister scene
luna.scene.update( dt : number )  -- Updates the update
```

### `DepthSorter`

Represents a depth sorter Lua object.

```lua
DepthSorter:add( callback : function, depth : number )  -- Registers a draw callback at the given depth layer. Higher `depth` values draw in front
DepthSorter:addObject( obj : table )  -- Registers a table object with a `draw` method at the given depth
DepthSorter:clear()  -- Removes all registered callbacks and objects without calling them
DepthSorter:flush()  -- Calls all registered draw callbacks and object `draw()` methods in sorted depth order, then clears the list
DepthSorter:getCount()  -- @return any
DepthSorter:sort()  -- Sorts all registered callbacks and objects by their depth values (ascending)
```

## `luna.postfx` {#postfx}

> Registers the `luna.postfx` table and exposes factory functions:
>
> - `luna.postfx.newEffect(name)` â€” creates a built-in `PostFxEffect`
>   by name (`"bloom"`, `"blur"`, `"crt"`, `"godrays"`, `"vignette"`,
>   `"colourgrade"`, `"chromatic"`).
> - `luna.postfx.newPass(shaderId)` â€” creates a `Custom` `PostFxEffect`
>   backed by an external shader resource ID.
> - `luna.postfx.newStack(width?, height?)` â€” creates an empty
>   `PostFxStack` with the given canvas dimensions.
> - `luna.postfx.newImageEffect(...)` â€” creates a per-image
>   [`LuaImageEffect`] chain (empty, single-effect, chain-table, or
>   options-table overloads).
> - `luna.postfx.loadImageEffect(path)` â€” loads a [`LuaImageEffect`]
>   chain from a TOML preset file.

*Coverage: 44/44 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.postfx.getEffectTypes() -> table<string>  -- Useful for introspection tools or mod loaders that enumerate
luna.postfx.loadImageEffect( path : string ) -> ImageEffect  -- Loads a per-image effect chain from a TOML preset file saved by `ImageEffect:save`
luna.postfx.newEffect( name : string ) -> PostFxEffect  -- Creates a built-in post-processing effect by name
luna.postfx.newImageEffect() -> ImageEffect  -- Creates a per-image effect chain
luna.postfx.newPass( shader_id : integer ) -> PostFxEffect  -- Creates a custom shader pass effect
luna.postfx.newStack()  -- Creates and returns a new stack instance
```

### `ImageEffect`

Represents a image effect Lua object.

```lua
ImageEffect:addEffect() -> PostFxEffect  -- Appends a new effect pass by name to the end of this effect chain
ImageEffect:clearEffects()  -- Removes all effects from this chain
ImageEffect:clone() -> ImageEffect  -- The returned object shares no state with the original
ImageEffect:effectCount() -> integer  -- # Returns
ImageEffect:getEffect()  -- When the first argument is an integer, retrieves by 1-based position
ImageEffect:removeEffect() -> boolean  -- Removes an effect by 1-based index or by type name
ImageEffect:save()  -- Serialises this effect chain to a TOML file at the given path
```

### `PostFxEffect`

Represents a post fx effect Lua object.

```lua
PostFxEffect:getEffectType() -> string  -- Alias for `getType`. Provided for API consistency â€” both names
PostFxEffect:getParameterNames()  -- The returned Lua table is 1-indexed and sorted alphabetically
PostFxEffect:getType() -> string  -- Corresponds to `PostFxEffectType::name()`. Returns `"custom"` for
PostFxEffect:hasParameter() -> boolean  -- Useful for introspection and serialisation. Custom effects start
PostFxEffect:isBuiltIn() -> boolean  -- # Returns
PostFxEffect:isEnabled() -> boolean  -- Disabled effects are skipped by the stack during `apply()` without
PostFxEffect:setBrightness()  -- Applied as a uniform scale across all channels before contrast
PostFxEffect:setContrast()  -- Pivots around mid-grey (0.5). Values above 1.0 push darks darker
PostFxEffect:setEnabled()  -- Enables or disables this effect within its parent stack
PostFxEffect:setIntensity()  -- For bloom: scales the brightness of the blurred highlight layer
PostFxEffect:setOffset()  -- The R, G, and B channels are sampled at offsets proportional to
PostFxEffect:setParameter()  -- Inserts or replaces the parameter keyed by `name`. Common parameter
PostFxEffect:setRadius()  -- Larger values spread the blur sample footprint across more pixels
PostFxEffect:setSaturation()  -- 0.0 produces a greyscale image; 1.0 is neutral; values above 1.0
PostFxEffect:setScanlineStrength()  -- Controls how prominent the dark horizontal scanlines are in the
PostFxEffect:setStrength()  -- For vignette: 0.0 is invisible; 1.0 darkens the corners to black
PostFxEffect:setThreshold()  -- Pixels with luminance above this threshold contribute to the bloom
```

### `PostFxStack`

Represents a post fx stack Lua object.

```lua
PostFxStack:add()  -- Appends a `PostFxEffect` to the end of the stack
PostFxStack:apply()  -- Applies all enabled effects in the stack
PostFxStack:beginCapture()  -- Starts a post-processing capture pass
PostFxStack:endCapture()  -- Ends a post-processing capture pass
PostFxStack:getDimensions()  -- # Returns
PostFxStack:getEffect()  -- Provides random access into the chain. Returns the
PostFxStack:getEffectCount() -> integer  -- Useful for iterating via `getEffect(i)` from Lua
PostFxStack:getEnabledEffects()  -- Used by the GPU layer during `apply()` to determine which shader
PostFxStack:getHeight() -> integer  -- # Returns
PostFxStack:getWidth() -> integer  -- # Returns
PostFxStack:isCapturing() -> boolean  -- Useful for guard checks in Lua to avoid nested capture calls
PostFxStack:isEffectEnabled() -> boolean  -- Queries the per-position enabled flag in the chain. Returns `false`
PostFxStack:remove() -> boolean  -- Removes a `PostFxEffect` from the stack chain
PostFxStack:resize()  -- Resizes the internal canvas dimensions
```

## `luna.debugbridge` {#debugbridge}

> Registers the `luna.debugbridge.*` TCP debug server API.
>
> Embeds a JSON-over-TCP server (127.0.0.1 only) inside the running game.
> External tools (VS Code extension, MCP server) connect to inspect and
> control the game at runtime. Network I/O runs on a background thread;
> Lua-dependent methods are queued and dispatched via `poll()` on the main thread.

*Coverage: 14/15 items documented (93%)*

```lua
luna.debugbridge.broadcast( event : string, json_data : string )  -- Broadcasts a JSON event to all connected clients
luna.debugbridge.capturePrint( msg : string, source : string?, line : integer? )  -- Captures a print message and broadcasts it to connected clients
luna.debugbridge.clearPrintHistory()
luna.debugbridge.getClientCount()  -- @return any
luna.debugbridge.getPerformance() -> table  -- @return table
luna.debugbridge.getPort()  -- @return any
luna.debugbridge.getPrintHistory( count : integer? ) -> table  -- @param count : integer?
luna.debugbridge.isRunning()  -- @return any
luna.debugbridge.isScreenshotRequested()  -- @return any
luna.debugbridge.poll()  -- Poll for pending Lua-dependent requests from TCP clients
luna.debugbridge.recordFrame( dt : number )  -- Records a frame time sample
luna.debugbridge.requestScreenshot( scale : integer? )  -- Flags a screenshot request for the next frame
luna.debugbridge.setMaxPrintHistory( max : integer )  -- @param max : integer
luna.debugbridge.start( port : u16? ) -> boolean  -- Start the TCP debug server on 127.0.0.1:port
luna.debugbridge.stop()  -- Stop the TCP debug server and close all connections
```

---

## `luna.docs` {#docs}

> Registers the `luna.docs.*` documentation management API.

```lua
luna.docs.exportAll( catalog_ud : userdata, output_dir : string )  -- Export all three files to a directory
luna.docs.exportCheatsheet( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export one-line-per-function cheatsheet
luna.docs.exportCompletions( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export completions JSON for VS Code IntelliSense
luna.docs.exportHover( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export hover JSON
luna.docs.exportMarkdown( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export markdown API reference
luna.docs.exportSignatures( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export signatures JSON

luna.docs.loadAll( directory : string )  -- Load all .toml files in a directory and merge
luna.docs.loadToml( path : string )  -- Load a TOML doc file into an ApiCatalog
luna.docs.quality( catalog_ud : userdata? )  -- Calculate quality metrics for a catalog
luna.docs.qualityModule( module_name : string, catalog_ud : userdata? )  -- Quality for a single module
luna.docs.resetCatalog()  -- Reset the internal catalog
luna.docs.scan( opts : table? )  -- Scan the luna.* namespace to build an API catalog from live bindings
luna.docs.scanModule( module_name : string )  -- Scan a single module's bindings
luna.docs.setParamInfo( qualified_name : string, params : table )  -- Set parameter info for an entry
luna.docs.setReturnInfo( qualified_name : string, returns : table )  -- Set return type info for an entry
luna.docs.validate( catalog_ud : userdata? )  -- Validate catalog completeness against live bindings
```

## `luna.patterns` {#patterns}

> Registers the `luna.patterns.*` software design patterns API.
>
> Provides factory functions and UserData wrappers for reusable patterns:
> EventBus (prioritized pub-sub), ObjectPool (object reuse), CommandStack
> (undo/redo), ServiceLocator (DI container), Factory (named constructors),
> and SimpleState (finite state machine).

*Coverage: 47/47 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.patterns.newCommandStack() -> CommandStack  -- New command stack
luna.patterns.newEventBus() -> EventBus  -- Creates and returns a new event bus instance
luna.patterns.newFactory() -> Factory  -- Creates and returns a new factory instance
luna.patterns.newObjectPool() -> ObjectPool  -- New object pool
luna.patterns.newServiceLocator() -> ServiceLocator  -- New service locator
luna.patterns.newSimpleState() -> SimpleState  -- New simple state
```

### `CommandStack`

Represents a command stack Lua object.

```lua
CommandStack:canRedo() -> integer  -- @return integer
CommandStack:canUndo() -> boolean  -- @return boolean
CommandStack:clearAll()  -- Clear all on this CommandStack
CommandStack:execute( name : string, exec_fn : function, undo_fn : function? )  -- Execute on this CommandStack
CommandStack:getCurrentName()  -- # Returns
CommandStack:getHistorySize() -> integer  -- @return integer
CommandStack:redo() -> boolean  -- Redo on this CommandStack
CommandStack:undo() -> boolean  -- Undo on this CommandStack
```

### `EventBus`

Represents a event bus Lua object.

```lua
EventBus:clear( event : string )  -- Removes all entries
EventBus:clearAll()  -- Clear all on this EventBus
EventBus:emit( args : MultiValue )  -- @param args : MultiValue
EventBus:getEvents()  -- @return any
EventBus:getListenerCount( event : string )  -- @param event : string
EventBus:off( id : integer )  -- Removes a previously registered event listener
EventBus:on( event : string, callback : function, priority : integer? )  -- Registers an event listener callback
```

### `Factory`

Represents a factory Lua object.

```lua
Factory:clearAll()  -- Clear all on this Factory
Factory:create( args : MultiValue )  -- Creates a new Factory instance

```

## `luna.font` {#font}

> Lua API bindings for the `luna.font.*` font rasterizer module.
>
> Provides `newRasterizer`, `newTrueTypeRasterizer`, `newBMFontRasterizer`,
> and `newGlyphData` factory functions matching the LÖVE2D `love.font` module
> surface.  Font loading delegates to the same `Font::from_bytes` path used by
> `luna.graphics.newFont` so the returned `LuaFont` userdata is fully
> compatible with all existing graphics API functions.

*Coverage: 11/11 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.font.newBMFontRasterizer()  -- Stub: BMFont rasterization is not yet supported
luna.font.newGlyphData()  -- `font` may be a `LuaFont` userdata or a raw font ID integer
luna.font.newRasterizer()  -- Loads a TTF/OTF font file and returns a font userdata object
luna.font.newTrueTypeRasterizer()  -- Loads a TTF/OTF font with an optional hinting hint (currently ignored)
```

## `luna.light` {#light}

> Registers the `luna.light.*` 2D lighting API.
>
> Provides Lua bindings for creating and managing 2D point lights
> and polygon shadow occluders via the `LightWorld` resource pool.

*Coverage: 19/74 items documented (26%)*

```lua
luna.light.advanceFlickers()  -- ── luna.light.advanceFlickers(dt) ──────────────────────────────
luna.light.clear()  -- ── luna.light.clear() ──────────────────────────────────────────
luna.light.getAmbient()  -- ── luna.light.getAmbient() ──────────────────────────────────────

luna.light.newLight()  -- ── luna.light.newLight(x, y, radius [, opts]) ────────────────────
luna.light.newOccluder()  -- ── luna.light.newOccluder(vertices [, opts]) ─────────────────────
luna.light.setAmbient()  -- ── luna.light.setAmbient(r, g, b [, a]) ─────────────────────────
luna.light.setEnabled()  -- ── luna.light.setEnabled(enabled) ───────────────────────────────
```

## `luna.network` {#network}

> Registers the `luna.network` namespace.
>
> Provides Lua-level UDP networking via ENet: host creation, peer
> management, packet sending and receiving. Wraps [`NetworkHost`].

*Coverage: 5/5 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.network.newHost() -> NetworkHost  -- Creates a new [`NetworkHost`] bound to the given address
```

### `NetworkHost`

Lua UserData wrapper for a [`NetworkHost`].

```lua
NetworkHost:disconnect()  -- Gracefully disconnects a peer. The disconnect event arrives on the next `service()`
NetworkHost:flush()  -- Flushes pending sends immediately
NetworkHost:getAddress() -> string  -- @return string
NetworkHost:service() -> table|nil  -- Polls the network for one event. Returns an event table or `nil`
```

---

## `luna.overlay` {#overlay}

> Registers the `luna.overlay` table and exposes a single factory:
>
> - `luna.overlay.newOverlay(width?, height?)` â€” creates a `LuaOverlay`
>   UserData object.

*Coverage: 57/57 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.overlay.newOverlay()  -- Creates and returns a new overlay instance
```

### `Overlay`

Represents a overlay Lua object.

```lua
Overlay:clear()  -- Resets all overlay subsystems to their inactive defaults
Overlay:draw()  -- Placeholder for draw â€” actual rendering handled by the game loop
Overlay:getAmbientColor() -> number  -- When time-of-day cycling is active the returned value may change
Overlay:getCloudCount() -> number  -- @return number
Overlay:getCloudOpacity() -> number  -- @return number
Overlay:getCloudScale() -> number  -- @return number
Overlay:getCloudSpeed() -> number  -- @return number
Overlay:getDimensions() -> number  -- Convenience function; equivalent to calling `getWidth()` and
Overlay:getFilmGrainIntensity() -> number  -- @return number
Overlay:getFogColor() -> number  -- @return number, number, number, number
Overlay:getFogDensity() -> number  -- @return number
Overlay:getHeatHazeIntensity() -> number  -- @return number
Overlay:getHeight() -> number  -- Reflects the value passed to `newOverlay` or the most recent
Overlay:getLightningColor() -> number  -- @return number, number, number, number
Overlay:getShakeOffset() -> number  -- offset all draw operations by this pair to apply the shake
Overlay:getTimeOfDay() -> number  -- @return number
Overlay:getVignetteStrength() -> number  -- @return number
Overlay:getWeather() -> string  -- @return string
Overlay:getWeatherIntensity() -> number  -- @return number
Overlay:getWidth() -> number  -- Reflects the value passed to `newOverlay` or the most recent
Overlay:getWindDirection() -> number  -- @return number
Overlay:getWindSpeed() -> number  -- @return number
Overlay:isActive() -> boolean  -- Checks weather enabled, ambient enabled, flash/shake/fade/lightning
Overlay:isAmbientEnabled() -> boolean  -- @return boolean
Overlay:isCloudShadowsEnabled() -> boolean  -- @return boolean
Overlay:isFading() -> boolean  -- @return boolean
Overlay:isFilmGrainEnabled() -> boolean  -- @return boolean
Overlay:isFlashing() -> boolean  -- @return boolean
Overlay:isFogEnabled() -> boolean  -- @return boolean
Overlay:isHeatHazeEnabled() -> boolean  -- @return boolean
Overlay:isShaking() -> boolean  -- @return boolean
Overlay:isVignetteEnabled() -> boolean  -- @return boolean
Overlay:isWeatherEnabled() -> boolean  -- @return boolean
Overlay:resize( width : number, height : number )  -- Updates the internal canvas dimensions on window resize
Overlay:setAmbientEnabled( enabled : boolean )  -- Enables or disables automatic time-of-day ambient colour cycling
Overlay:setCloudCount( count : number )  -- Higher counts produce denser, more uniform coverage. Each blob
Overlay:setCloudOpacity( opacity : number )  -- Controls how darkly the shadow blobs are blended over the scene
Overlay:setCloudScale( scale : number )  -- 1.0 is the default blob size; smaller values produce compact
Overlay:setCloudShadows( enabled : boolean )  -- Enables or disables the scrolling cloud shadow overlay
Overlay:setCloudSpeed( speed : number )  -- The internal `offset` accumulator grows without bound; the renderer
Overlay:setFilmGrainEnabled( enabled : boolean )  -- Enables or disables per-frame random film grain noise
Overlay:setFilmGrainIntensity( intensity : number )  -- 0.1â€“0.3 gives a subtle cinematic look. Values above 0.5
Overlay:setFogDensity( density : number )  -- Maps directly to the alpha of the fog colour overlay
Overlay:setFogEnabled( enabled : boolean )  -- Enables or disables atmospheric fog
Overlay:setHeatHazeEnabled( enabled : boolean )  -- Enables or disables UV-distortion heat shimmer
Overlay:setHeatHazeIntensity( intensity : number )  -- Controls the peak UV displacement in pixels. 0.2â€“2.0 gives
Overlay:setTimeOfDay( hour : number )  -- The value is in 24-hour floating-point format (0.0 = midnight,
Overlay:setVignetteEnabled( enabled : boolean )  -- Enables or disables screen-edge darkening (vignette)
Overlay:setVignetteStrength( strength : number )  -- 0.0 is invisible; 0.5 is a gentle filmic border; 1.0 crushes
Overlay:setWeather( weather_type : string )  -- Valid names: `"none"`, `"rain"`, `"snow"`, `"hail"`, `"dust"`,
Overlay:setWeatherEnabled( enabled : boolean )  -- Enables or disables the weather particle spawner
Overlay:setWeatherIntensity( intensity : number )  -- Controls both the maximum particle count (up to 200) and the
Overlay:setWindDirection( angle : number )  -- 0.0 blows particles to the right; `math.pi / 2` blows downward
Overlay:setWindSpeed( speed : number )  -- Adds a global velocity offset to every live weather particle each
Overlay:triggerLightning()  -- Triggers a one-shot lightning flash
Overlay:update( dt : number )  -- Advances all active overlay subsystems by `dt` seconds
```

## `luna.procgen` {#procgen}

> Registers the `luna.procgen` namespace.
>
> Exposes stateless procedural generation utilities: cellular-automata
> cave maps, BFS flood fill, Poisson disk sampling, periodic Perlin noise,
> and Voronoi diagrams.

*Coverage: 5/5 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.procgen.cellularAutomata() -> table  -- Generates a cave-like map using cellular automata
luna.procgen.floodFill() -> table  -- BFS flood fill on a flat grid of bytes
luna.procgen.perlinNoise() -> number  -- Evaluates a single periodic Perlin noise value at `(x, y)`
luna.procgen.poissonDisk() -> table  -- Generates Poisson disk sample points using Bridson's algorithm
luna.procgen.voronoi() -> table  -- Generates a Voronoi diagram for a set of seed points
```

## `luna.serial` {#serial}

> Registers the `luna.serial.*` format serialization API.
>
> Provides `luna.serial` with functions to parse and serialize JSON, TOML, and CSV
> to/from Lua tables. YAML removed — use TOML for config (design-assumption B-05).
> No file I/O — strings in, strings out.

*Coverage: 6/6 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.serial.fromCsv( s : string, delimiter : string?, has_headers : boolean? )  -- Parses a CSV string and returns a Lua table (sequence of row tables)
luna.serial.fromJson( s : string )  -- Parses a JSON string and returns a Lua table
luna.serial.fromToml( s : string )  -- Parses a TOML string and returns a Lua table
luna.serial.toCsv( table : table, delimiter : string?, has_headers : boolean? )  -- Serializes a Lua table (sequence of row tables) to a CSV string
luna.serial.toJson( table : table, pretty : boolean? )  -- Serializes a Lua table to a JSON string
luna.serial.toToml( table : table )  -- Serializes a Lua table to a TOML string
```

## `luna.terminal` {#terminal}

> Lua API bindings for the `luna.terminal.*` text-mode terminal emulator.
>
> This binding layer uses userdata handles so terminal methods work with
> idiomatic Lua colon syntax. Widget constructors return detached widget
> handles that carry their own data and callbacks until `addWidget()`
> attaches them to a terminal.

*Coverage: 18/55 items documented (33%)*
